channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: MG Unveils Cyber and GO! Electric Concepts for Future SUVs and Hatchbacks
item.description: MG has unveiled two new electric concept cars at the Goodwood Festival of Speed, showcasing its future design direction for SUVs and hatchbacks. The MG Cyber Concept previews a potential future model ...

item.title: Senegal Fires Coach Pape Thiaw After Poor Results, Seeks New Leader
item.description: The Senegalese Football Federation has dismissed head coach Pape Thiaw after his brief tenure with the national team failed to deliver expected results. Thiaw, who took charge recently, faced mounting...

item.title: Philippines upholds 2016 South China Sea ruling amid diplomatic efforts
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. reaffirmed the Philippines' commitment to the 2016 arbitral ruling that invalidated China's South China Sea claims, contrasting it with former President Rodri...

item.title: Celebrities, Family News, and Sports Highlights: A Mixed Bag of Updates
item.description: The article compiles various news snippets covering celebrity gossip, family matters, and sports events. It includes details about Rosie O'Donnell's visit to her incarcerated daughter, the death of Ma...

item.title: Sorloth's Decision to Shoot Instead of Passing to Haaland Costs Norway World Cup Match
item.description: Alexander Sorloth, Norway's striker, explained his decision to shoot instead of passing to Erling Haaland during the World Cup quarter-final against England. Norway led 1-0 when Sorloth received the b...

item.title: San Francisco's Drone Surveillance and Privacy Concerns
item.description: A recent investigation into San Francisco's use of drones for surveillance reveals significant privacy risks and policy gaps. The article highlights how the city's drone program, which includes tracki...

item.title: Kisumu Gang Violence Results in Two Deaths and 200 Arrests
item.description: Two young men lost their lives after rival gangs clashed in Kisumu's Milimani area, leaving a motor vehicle and nine motorcycles destroyed. Kisumu Governor Anyang Nyong'o confirmed eight suspects are ...

item.title: Cotabato City Enhances Security Measures Following Three Ambushes
item.description: Cotabato City authorities have intensified security measures following three consecutive ambushes that left three people dead and six others injured. The incidents occurred in three different barangay...

item.title: Mitch McConnell's Proof of Life and One Nation's Media Ties Amid Cricket Controversy
item.description: The article explores the legacy of former senator and media personality Derryn Hinch, who passed away recently, highlighting tributes from across the political spectrum and a detailed obituary in The ...

item.title: NBI investigation finds no evidence of VP Duterte’s alleged hitman
item.description: During the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte, National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) Regional Director Jeremy Lotoc testified that his team found no validated information to identify th...

item.title: Madras High Court allows educationist Ishari K. Ganesh to withdraw plea against money laundering case, permits future approach
item.description: The Madras High Court on July 13, 2026, permitted educationist and film producer Ishari K. Ganesh to withdraw his writ petition filed in 2025 to quash a money laundering case under the Prevention of M...

item.title: MS NOW panel criticizes Trump administration over subpoenas tied to reporting on Qatari aircraft security concerns
item.description: A discussion on MSNBC's 'Morning Joe' focused on the Trump administration's decision to subpoena New York Times reporters following their reporting on security vulnerabilities involving a luxury aircr...

item.title: Ezza Youth Group Rejects Zoning, Supports Odii for 2027 Governorship
item.description: The Ezza Ezekuna Youth Assembly (EEYA) has rejected Ebonyi State's zoning arrangement for the governorship, arguing that competence and credibility should determine leadership. During a gathering in O...

item.title: Tanzania halts steel plant operations due to environmental and security issues
item.description: The government of Tanzania has ordered the indefinite closure of Hangjin Steel Tanzania Limited in the Coast Region after discovering severe environmental violations and unexplained ammunition at the ...

item.title: ned admission test highlights major flaws in sindh's public examination system
item.description: the results of the ned university admission test have exposed significant issues with academic standards in sindh's public examination boards. students from foreign and cambridge examination systems a...

item.title: Experts Warn About the Health Impacts of Prolonged Cross-Legged Sitting
item.description: Sitting with crossed legs is a common posture, but experts caution that prolonged use can lead to postural imbalances and muscle weaknesses. Physical therapists note that while this position provides ...

item.title: Community and Union Push Back Against Proposed Art Park Near City Hall
item.description: A two-acre vacant lot near Los Angeles City Hall is set to become an 'El Corazón Art Park' leased by AltaMed Health Services for $175,000 through 2027. The project, which includes an art gallery, 30 b...

item.title: Protest Vote Against Pauline Hanson Risks National Stability
item.description: The article features letters to the editor discussing the potential consequences of voting for Pauline Hanson's One Nation party as a protest vote. Contributors argue that such a decision could lead t...

item.title: Unifor starts ratification process for potential Ford labor agreement
item.description: The article discusses Unifor, Canada's largest labour union, beginning ratification meetings on Friday for a tentative agreement with Ford Motor Company. The deal, which includes wage increases and im...

item.title: World Cup shifts focus to top players after underdogs exit
item.description: The article discusses the transition of focus in the World Cup from underdog teams to leading players like Lionel Messi, Kylian Mbappé, and Jude Bellingham. It highlights Erling Haaland's disappointin...
